item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just wanted to belatedly note I replaced Maders with amnestics. I wanted to do something different for the short works contest to stand out but it doesn't make much sense now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From the reading, there is NO difference, the author just decided to rename them and invent a 'Foundation Style Guide'. It kinda bugged me seeing that too, there doesn't seem to be any reason to replace the term 'amnestic' with an acronym that's chunkier and needs so much explanation.

Also, what is a 'general-purpose SCP document'? This aside really makes the whole thing worse as an article, I feel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Memory-Altering Drugs (sometimes colloquially referred to as "maders"). The 2010 Foundation Style Guide recommends the use of the term "MaDr" over "amnestic" in general-purpose SCP documents to avoid confusion.

While re-reading this again, I must ask: What is this about? How are MaDr different from amnestics?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It felt really weird seeing an SCP about a pup I pass by almost daily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is premised on the whole Sherlock Holmes thing, but there's nothing of that concept in this aside from the most literal take on it. None of the character or personality of it comes through. There's not even any deductive reasoning involved. It just catches criminals, and that misses much of the essence of the work that you're choosing to construct this on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doing the second round of all SC and all my comments…

I did say I would not no-vote on an SC, so I've decided on downvote. This skip doesn't do much for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a bad article, but I feel it's a little too on-the-nose what with ACD and all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you look closely, you'll notice I never out-and-out stated who the author of Sherlock Holmes was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Surely the Foundation view it's task as the containment of any magical objects, not just dangerous or indiscreet ones. There's nothing threatening about a concrete man of a bird in the shape of an aeroplane, but they're contained on principle.

As for the article, I like the idea, but the writing belabour's the joke IMO. Do Foundation personnel really need to be reminded who wrote the Holmes stories?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmmh. <scratches head vigorously> I'm of two minds about this. It's nice, but… why should the Foundation prevent people from entering? Why should it care? SCP-2475 is discreet about its anomalous effect… Or am I missing something? No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhhhhhhhhhhh. It's a really narrow downvote, but downvote nonetheless. The Sir Arthur Conan Doyle reference seems to be the only main factor behind this, and while the idea of Sherlock Holmes calling in crime solutions is fairly amusing, there's just not enough done with it in the space given (obviously a limitation of the contest itself) to make it more interesting for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros:

  • Wow, this one is odd and I can really enjoy the concept behind it.
  • Following LordSpy's comments on its comic value, it is done via a serious veil that I took it as that initially.

Cons:

  • I am questioning the mention of its use for law enforcement. 2475 seems to be interested in its own sense of justice.

Verdict: Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing I'm really enjoying about this contest is how much range is being done in terms of expected reaction. This one was comic whilst still remaining within margins of a "serious" mainlister in that we really don't know what is fueling these actions, even if they point out to a certain fictional someone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just remembered who Conan Doyle is.

+1

item.title: Non-Author's Note
item.content:encoded: I edited in ObserverSeptember's entry for him, and fixed a typo he had.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Since the contest posting duration lasts for around three days, I'm more amazed at how one sleeps for that long.

On a serious note, hope to see your entry.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
As per contest rules, posting this ahead of time because I will likely be asleep during the submission period.

Please do not vote on this entry until it has been posted by staff. Thanks!

EDIT: Thanks to Decibelle for posting this for me.

EDIT 2: Picture was taken by me and is released under CC 3.0
